full_text:
Thesis presentation
Profiling of Spatial Tumor Microenvironment Architecture in Highly Multiplexed Images of High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma

Alva Grönholm

Exploring spatial patterns in the TME can provide valuable clinical insights

Spatial analysis uncovers recurrent cellular neighborhoods in the TME

Spatial analysis uncovers recurrent cellular neighborhoods in the TME

Aim of the thesis

Compare two spatial analysis methods: Spatial count and LDA
Assess how each method captures and defines recurrent cellular neighborhoods (RCNs)
Analyze the impact of spatial scale in each method
Evaluate each method's ability to detect clinically relevant spatial patterns

14 markers revealed 11 phenotypes across 6,5 million cells

6

Composition analysis highlights distinct TME profiles across patients

Cell-type size variation influences neighbor identification, highlighting the impact of radius choice

Cell-type size variation influences neighbor identification, highlighting the impact of radius choice

Spatial count reveals scale-dependent shifts

Spatial count reveals scale-dependent shifts

LDA preserves spatial coherence

CD8+

CD4+

Speaker notes:
Spatial Count
Captures scale-sensitive shifts in cellular composition.
Small radius (13 µm): Detects fine, localized structures (e.g., vessels, TLS).
Larger radius (25–35 µm): Integrates into broader, heterogeneous tissue environments.
More RCN diversity, especially at small scales.
LDA
Maintains spatial coherence across radii.
Stable immune RCNs (e.g., CD4+, macrophages).
Less sensitive to scale, better at retaining fine-grained structure.
Lower RCN diversity, but more homogeneous and robust.

LDA is more homogenous than spatial count

Spatial count and LDA reveal similar RCN enrichment linked to clinical outcome

Conclusion: No one-size-fits-all

Small radius: fine-grained, cell-level interactions
Large radius: broader tissue architectures

Aspect | Spatial Count | LDA
Sensitivity to spatial scale | Scale-dependent | Robust
Interpretation | More straight-forward | More abstract
Diversity | High | Low
Aggregation pattern | Localized | Disaggregated
